system.description: An arrow is a lightweight wooden shaft tipped with a metal point, used as ammunition for bows. Its feathered fletching ensures stable flight, allowing it to strike targets accurately at range.

A light crossbow bolt is a short, narrow projectile designed for use with light crossbows, optimized for speed and accuracy. Its compact weight allows for quick reloading and consistent performance in ranged combat.

A heavy crossbow bolt is a thicker, sturdier projectile built for high-powered crossbows, delivering deep penetration even against armored foes. Its reinforced shaft and weight make it slower to load but devastating on impact.

A silver-tipped arrow is a standard arrow fitted with a finely crafted silver head instead of iron or steel. It is prized for its effectiveness against supernatural creatures vulnerable to silver, making it a valuable tool for hunters and adventurers.

A sling stone is a smooth, oval projectile shaped for optimal release from a sling's pouch. It is commonly used by hunters and skirmishers for its simplicity, low cost, and surprising lethality when launched at high speed.
